search_text: Bayesian deep learning framework for uncertainty quantification in high dimensions 用于高维不确定性量化的贝叶斯深度学习框架 We develop a novel deep learning method for uncertainty quantification in stochastic partial differential equations based on Bayesian neural network (BNN) and Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (HMC). A BNN efficiently learns the posterior distribution of the parameters in deep neural networks by performing Bayesian inference on the network parameters. The posterior distribution is efficiently sampled using HMC to quantify uncertainties in the system. Several numerical examples are shown for both forward and inverse problems in high dimension to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method for uncertainty quantification. These also show promising results that the computational cost is almost independent of the dimension of the problem demonstrating the potential of the method for tackling the so-called curse of dimensionality. 我们开发了一种基于贝叶斯神经网络（BNN）和哈密顿蒙特卡罗（HMC）的随机偏微分方程不确定性量化的新型深度学习方法。 BNN 通过对网络参数进行贝叶斯推理，有效地学习深度神经网络中参数的后验分布。使用 HMC 对后验分布进行有效采样，以量化系统中的不确定性。针对高维正向和逆向问题给出了几个数值示例，以证明所提出的不确定性量化方法的有效性。这些还显示出有希望的结果，即计算成本几乎与问题的维度无关，证明了该方法解决所谓的维度灾难的潜力。 本文提出一种基于贝叶斯神经网络和汉密尔顿蒙特卡洛方法的深度学习框架，用于高维随机偏微分方程的不确定性量化。 Jeahan Jung,Minseok Choi stat.ML,cs.AI,cs.LG

title: Analogical Concept Memory for Architectures Implementing the Common Model of Cognition
title_zh: 用于实现通用认知模型的架构的类比概念记忆
comment: Under review at Cognitive Systems Research. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2006.01962
comments: Under review at Cognitive Systems Research. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2006.01962
abstract: Architectures that implement the Common Model of Cognition - Soar, ACT-R, and Sigma - have a prominent place in research on cognitive modeling as well as on designing complex intelligent agents. In this paper, we explore how computational models of analogical processing can be brought into these architectures to enable concept acquisition from examples obtained interactively. We propose a new analogical concept memory for Soar that augments its current system of declarative long-term memories. We frame the problem of concept learning as embedded within the larger context of interactive task learning (ITL) and embodied language processing (ELP). We demonstrate that the analogical learning methods implemented in the proposed memory can quickly learn a diverse types of novel concepts that are useful not only in recognition of a concept in the environment but also in action selection. Our approach has been instantiated in an implemented cognitive system AILEEN and evaluated on a simulated robotic domain.
summary: Architectures that implement the Common Model of Cognition - Soar, ACT-R, and Sigma - have a prominent place in research on cognitive modeling as well as on designing complex intelligent agents. In this paper, we explore how computational models of analogical processing can be brought into these architectures to enable concept acquisition from examples obtained interactively. We propose a new analogical concept memory for Soar that augments its current system of declarative long-term memories. We frame the problem of concept learning as embedded within the larger context of interactive task learning (ITL) and embodied language processing (ELP). We demonstrate that the analogical learning methods implemented in the proposed memory can quickly learn a diverse types of novel concepts that are useful not only in recognition of a concept in the environment but also in action selection. Our approach has been instantiated in an implemented cognitive system AILEEN and evaluated on a simulated robotic domain.
abstract_zh: 实现通用认知模型的架构 - Soar、ACT-R 和 Sigma - 在认知建模以及设计复杂智能代理的研究中占有重要地位。在本文中，我们探讨了如何将类比处理的计算模型引入这些架构中，以便从交互式获得的示例中获取概念。我们为 Soar 提出了一种新的类比概念记忆，增强了其当前的陈述性长期记忆系统。我们将概念学习问题嵌入到交互式任务学习（ITL）和具身语言处理（ELP）的更大背景中。我们证明，在所提出的记忆中实施的类比学习方法可以快速学习各种类型的新颖概念，这些概念不仅可用于识别环境中的概念，而且还可用于动作选择。我们的方法已在实施的认知系统 AILEEN 中实例化，并在模拟机器人领域进行评估。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种类比概念记忆模块，用于增强认知架构（如Soar）的声明性长期记忆，以实现从交互示例中学习概念的能力。
AI.motivation: 现有认知架构（Soar、ACT-R、Sigma）缺乏类比处理机制，难以从交互任务和语言处理中高效学习新概念。
AI.method: 设计类比概念记忆模块，将其嵌入交互式任务学习（ITL）和具身语言处理（ELP）框架，并在认知系统AILEEN中实现与验证。
AI.result: 该方法能快速学习多种新型概念，支持环境识别与动作选择，在模拟机器人领域验证有效。
AI.conclusion: 类比记忆机制可提升认知架构的概念学习能力，为复杂智能代理的设计提供新思路。

authors_text: Surgan Jandial,Yash Khasbage,Arghya Pal,Vineeth N Balasubramanian,Balaji Krishnamurthy
title: Distilling the Undistillable: Learning from a Nasty Teacher
title_zh: 蒸馏不可蒸馏的东西：向讨厌的老师学习
comment: Published in main track of ECCV 2022, 17 pages with references, 5 figures, 6 tables
comments: Published in main track of ECCV 2022, 17 pages with references, 5 figures, 6 tables
abstract: The inadvertent stealing of private/sensitive information using Knowledge Distillation (KD) has been getting significant attention recently and has guided subsequent defense efforts considering its critical nature. Recent work Nasty Teacher proposed to develop teachers which can not be distilled or imitated by models attacking it. However, the promise of confidentiality offered by a nasty teacher is not well studied, and as a further step to strengthen against such loopholes, we attempt to bypass its defense and steal (or extract) information in its presence successfully. Specifically, we analyze Nasty Teacher from two different directions and subsequently leverage them carefully to develop simple yet efficient methodologies, named as HTC and SCM, which increase the learning from Nasty Teacher by upto 68.63% on standard datasets. Additionally, we also explore an improvised defense method based on our insights of stealing. Our detailed set of experiments and ablations on diverse models/settings demonstrate the efficacy of our approach.
summary: The inadvertent stealing of private/sensitive information using Knowledge Distillation (KD) has been getting significant attention recently and has guided subsequent defense efforts considering its critical nature. Recent work Nasty Teacher proposed to develop teachers which can not be distilled or imitated by models attacking it. However, the promise of confidentiality offered by a nasty teacher is not well studied, and as a further step to strengthen against such loopholes, we attempt to bypass its defense and steal (or extract) information in its presence successfully. Specifically, we analyze Nasty Teacher from two different directions and subsequently leverage them carefully to develop simple yet efficient methodologies, named as HTC and SCM, which increase the learning from Nasty Teacher by upto 68.63% on standard datasets. Additionally, we also explore an improvised defense method based on our insights of stealing. Our detailed set of experiments and ablations on diverse models/settings demonstrate the efficacy of our approach.
abstract_zh: 使用知识蒸馏（KD）无意中窃取私人/敏感信息最近受到了广泛关注，并考虑到其关键性质，指导了后续的防御工作。最近的工作“讨厌的老师”提议培养不能被攻击它的模型提炼或模仿的教师。然而，坏老师提供的保密承诺尚未得到充分研究，作为加强此类漏洞的进一步措施，我们尝试绕过其防御并成功窃取（或提取）其存在的信息。具体来说，我们从两个不同的方向分析 Nasty Teacher，然后仔细利用它们开发简单而有效的方法，称为 HTC 和 SCM，这将标准数据集上 Nasty Teacher 的学习率提高了 68.63%。此外，我们还根据我们对盗窃的见解探索了一种即兴防御方法。我们对不同模型/设置的详细实验和消融集证明了我们方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过开发新方法绕过'Nasty Teacher'的防御机制，成功从其模型中提取信息。
AI.motivation: 尽管'Nasty Teacher'被设计为防止知识蒸馏攻击，但其安全性尚未充分验证，存在信息泄露风险。
AI.method: 从两个方向分析'Nasty Teacher'，提出HTC和SCM两种高效方法，并改进防御策略。
AI.result: 在标准数据集上实现68.63%的学习效果提升，并验证了攻击与防御方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 现有防御机制存在漏洞，本文方法可有效绕过防御，同时为改进安全策略提供新思路。
